content.full_text:
May 17, 1991

Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

This letter requests written confirmation of oral advice you rendered on Wednesday regarding the Political Reform Act's new limitations on gifts and honoraria to local public officials. (Gov. Code § 89501.) Specifically, you advised me that section 89501 allows local elected officials to receive payments or reimbursements for the actual cost of travel in connection with speeches or appearances made within and outside California. The travel payment does not constitute either a gift, honoraria or income to the official. In other words, a local elected official may receive up to $1,000 in honoraria from a single source plus the actual costs of his or her travel to and from the speech, regardless of the location of the event.

In addition, you advised that the public official would report the payment for the travel expense on a special "travel schedule" of the Statement of Economic Interests which is currently being drafted by the FPPC.

If this letter does not correctly summarize your advice, please call me immediately at 415/389-6800. I look forward to receiving your written confirmation.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton
